name: noop_nonempty_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_noop_nonempty_input(data: Seq<u8>, i: usize, input: Seq<u8>)
    requires
        0 <= i + input.len() <= data.len() <= usize::MAX,
        input.len() > 0,
    ensures
        seq_splice(data, i, input) == data,
{
}
reason: If a non-empty write is a no-op, set_range never actually modifies data

name: splice_changes_length
code:
proof fn phi_2_splice_changes_length(data: Seq<u8>, i: usize, input: Seq<u8>)
    requires
        0 <= i + input.len() <= data.len() <= usize::MAX,
        input.len() > 0,
    ensures
        seq_splice(data, i, input).len() != data.len(),
{
}
reason: If splice changes the sequence length, the ensures clause (length preserved) would be contradictory

name: written_byte_mismatch
code:
proof fn phi_3_written_byte_mismatch(data: Seq<u8>, i: usize, input: Seq<u8>)
    requires
        0 <= i + input.len() <= data.len() <= usize::MAX,
        input.len() > 0,
    ensures
        seq_splice(data, i, input)[i as int] != input[0],
{
}
reason: If the byte at position i doesn't match input[0], the splice wrote incorrect data

name: prefix_corrupted
code:
proof fn phi_4_prefix_corrupted(data: Seq<u8>, i: usize, input: Seq<u8>)
    requires
        0 <= i + input.len() <= data.len() <= usize::MAX,
        i > 0,
    ensures
        exists|k: int| 0 <= k < i && seq_splice(data, i, input)[k] != data[k],
{
}
reason: If bytes before position i are modified, set_range corrupts data outside the target range

name: distinct_inputs_same_result
code:
proof fn phi_5_distinct_inputs_same_result(data: Seq<u8>, i: usize, input1: Seq<u8>, input2: Seq<u8>)
    requires
        0 <= i + input1.len() <= data.len() <= usize::MAX,
        0 <= i + input2.len() <= data.len() <= usize::MAX,
        input1.len() == input2.len(),
        input1.len() > 0,
        input1[0] != input2[0],
    ensures
        seq_splice(data, i, input1) == seq_splice(data, i, input2),
{
}
reason: If two inputs differing in the first byte produce identical splices, the spec ignores input content
